title: fix build err while reading from stdin

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *zhangguanzhang* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **vrothberg** You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @vrothberg` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@zhangguanzhang Thanks for taking this on, but please assign the issue to yourself if you're going to work on it.  I'll do so now.
Ugh, too many buttons, I was going to say @zhangguanzhang that you could not assign this to yourself after all.
I have found the cause of the problem, and I will push to fix it later

It works ok
```shell
$ /zgz# echo -e "FROM debian:testing\nRUN /bin/echo 'Test'" | ./podman/bin/podman build -t debian-test -  
STEP 1: FROM debian:testing
STEP 2: RUN /bin/echo 'Test'
Test
STEP 3: COMMIT debian-test
--> b9a6fcc86fa
b9a6fcc86fa6539d71ce82e63a92bf905726cafe2d37ca45939fff46e851fd37
```
but this unit test reading standard input to build Docker image issue, which method should I use to write for it
@zhangguanzhang I'm not sure which test you're having issues with?  Did you see @rhatdan 's #7121
@rhatdan seems to have a more intuitive solution than mine, if his code merges please close this pr, or I will close this pr
@zhangguanzhang although this didn't get merged, I very much appreciate the quick response to the problem and your efforts to solve the problem!
